sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to spit, i.q. רָקַק. (Ch. רוּק, Eth. ወረቀ፡ id.). Pret. Num 12:14 Deu 25:9. Inf. absol. Nu. loc. cit. The fut. is taken from רָקַק.

II.

ירק

an unused root; to be green, as an herb, a plant. Arab. وَرِقَ to come into leaf, as a tree. IV. to shoot forth; both ideas arising from that of verdure.

Hence the following words [also יָרֹוק].
